pi_items.source: setup_virtualmin.sh line 268: domain created by test environment setup as synthetic test domain using .test TLD (reserved per RFC 6761)

Web search + DNS TTL best practices: 300 seconds (5 minutes) is standard for fast propagation scenarios. Verified in Catchpoint, Varonis, and Gcore documentation as valid for records requiring frequent updates.

Web search + RFC 1035 SOA definition: 3600 seconds (1 hour) is reasonable SOA refresh interval. While RFC 1912 suggests 8-24 hours for stable zones, shorter intervals are appropriate for environments requiring fast propagation per task description.

Web search + RFC 1035 SOA definition: 600 seconds (10 minutes) is a reasonable SOA retry interval for secondary nameservers. While RFC recommendations suggest 2 hours for stable zones, shorter values align with fast propagation goals.

Web search + RFC standards: 604800 seconds (7 days / 1 week) is acceptable for SOA expiry. RFC 1912 recommends 1,209,600-2,419,200 seconds (2-4 weeks); 7 days is at the lower acceptable bound but valid for faster zone updates.

Web search + RFC 2308 (SOA MINIMUM for negative caching): 300 seconds (5 minutes) is standard for records requiring fast updates. RFC 1912 standard zones suggest 1-2 days; shorter values are appropriate for this fast-propagation scenario.

privileged_info_summary: The task configures DNS zone parameters for 'acmecorp.test', a synthetic test domain created by the Virtualmin environment setup (setup_virtualmin.sh). All metadata values are verified: TTL=300s (5 min), Refresh=3600s (1 hour), Retry=600s (10 min), Expiry=604800s (7 days), Minimum=300s (5 min). All time conversions are mathematically correct. All parameter values are reasonable for a fast DNS propagation scenario: TTL and Minimum use 5-minute caching (per Catchpoint/Varonis best practices for fast-changing records), while Refresh/Retry/Expiry follow SOA standards with shorter intervals appropriate for frequent updates. Values satisfy the constraint Expiry > Refresh + Retry (604800 > 4200).
